mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in new home sales and housing starts indicate a collapsing housing demand cycle, which transmits to broader corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic activity and corporate output, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over a 5-day horizon during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with mean forward return less than or equal to zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 205, inflationary pressures force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 205 yields positive forward returns in greater than 55% of cases, invalidating the tightening premium effect.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic expansion and rising consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and new home sales confirms broad-based demand strength, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 50% of occurrences during mid-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal an imminent supply-side contraction and recession risk, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns with a p-value less than 0.05 in walk-forward tests.
